claim: EN 125 (Vila do Bispo to Vila Real de Santo António) is a 1st-class national road that runs the full east-west length of the Algarve coast — Lagos, Portimão, Lagoa, Faro, Olhão, Tavira — and is the primary road barrier dividing coastal from inland zones.
stance_target: road barrier and attractor classification for Algarve coastal corridor
quoted_support: EN 125 (wikipedia) Vila do Bispo – Vila Real de Santo António 6832045 (i J) Vila do Bispo – Lagos – Portimão – Lagoa – Faro – Olhão – Tavira – Vila Real de Santo António

claim: EN 124 (Portimão to Alcoutim, no OSM relation defined) runs through the interior Serra do Caldeirão — Portimão, Lagos port, Silves, São Bartolomeu de Messines, Barranco Velho, Cachopo, Alcoutim — and represents the main north–south interior corridor through high-hazard terrain.
stance_target: interior road network encoding for lost dog movement in Serra do Caldeirão
scope_conditions: Algarve interior, OSM relation not yet defined
quoted_support: EN 124 (wikipedia) Portimão – Alcoutim (proximidades) Relação não definida Portimão – porto de Lagos – Silves – São Bartolomeu de Messines – Barranco Velho – Cachopo – Alcoutim (proximidades)

claim: EN 2 (Chaves to Faro, OSM relation 7362083) is a 1st-class national road running the full north–south length of Portugal terminating in Faro — it enters the Algarve from the north through Castro Verde and represents a major transport vector for dogs traveling long distances.
stance_target: north–south transport vector into Algarve for long-range lost dog movement
scope_conditions: Full Portugal, OSM relation 7362083, terminates in Faro
quoted_support: EN 2 (wikipedia) Chaves – Faro 7362083 (i J) Chaves – Vila Real – Lamego – Viseu – Santa Comba Dão – Góis – Sertã – Abrantes – Mora – Montemor-o-Novo – Torrão – Castro Verde – Faro

claim: Algarve has 9 regional roads (ER) that form the secondary network: ER 124, ER 125, ER 125.11, ER 266, ER 267, ER 268, ER 270, ER 396, and ER 2-6 — these connect the coast to the Serra de Monchique and eastern Barrocal.
stance_target: secondary road network encoding for Algarve territorial layer
scope_conditions: Algarve Região, OSM Portugal roads data
quoted_support: ER 267 (wikipedia) Aljezur – São Marcos da Serra 7353624 (i J) Aljezur (IC 4) – Monchique – Alferce – São Marcos da Serra (IC 1) ... ER 270 (wikipedia) Fonte de Boliqueime – Tavira 7353755 (i J) Fonte de Boliqueime – Loulé – São Brás de Alportel – Tavira (IP 1)

claim: Portugal has 164 national roads and 158 regional roads in the national classification system, but EN 124 (a key Algarve interior road) has no OSM relation defined, indicating a data gap.
stance_target: OSM data quality assessment for Algarve road network
scope_conditions: OSM Portugal road data as of 2026
quoted_support: Existem ao todo 164 rodovias classificadas como estradas nacionais e 158 como regionais. ... EN 124 (wikipedia) Portimão – Alcoutim (proximidades) Relação não definida
